key_insight: MADA-RL improves compact-model mathematical reasoning by training generator and critic agents with a counterfactual, debate-aware reward that explicitly teaches critics to outperform generator consensus.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a parameter-efficient post-training path for small models, using LoRA and multi-agent coordination to gain accuracy with far fewer trainable parameters than full fine-tuning.
method: Split a compact model into generator and critic roles.
Train with a counterfactual critic advantage: critic reward minus generator ensemble accuracy.
Use LoRA adapters and a lightweight multi-round debate protocol at inference.
evidence_signal: Across five math benchmarks, DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-1.5B improved from 39.9% to 41.9% (+2.0 points, p<0.001) with 16× fewer trainable parameters than fully fine-tuned baselines.
watch_item: The gains do not surpass the strongest larger-data baselines, and the multi-round deployment protocol adds inference-time cost that must be budgeted in practice.
paper_title: MADA-RL: Multi-Agent Debate-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Parameter-Efficient Reasoning in Compact Models
